display_name: UCC sign-in screens — Figma representation + Internal-deferral policy amendment

research.note: Research inherited from parent ucc-passkey-auth. The decision to grant a form-driven exception to control-room components was made in the 2026-05-16 UU3/UU4/UU6 review.

prd.note: PRD inherited from parent ucc-passkey-auth. This enhancement adds Figma representation to already-shipped screens (no new product behavior).

tasks.approval_note: User approved 2026-05-17 with directive to review infra master plan first for data-integrity concerns. Review confirmed UU4 is calibration-window-safe (no infra-glob path touches, no new candidate streams in gate-coverage.jsonl). Two separate items DEFERRED to 2026-05-22 (post v7.9 promotion): Option C MEMORY.md staleness check (C11) + preflight enhancement_parent bug fix (C12).

ux_or_integration.skip_reason: work_type:enhancement (screens already live in production; this work documents existing UI in Figma, no new UX)

implementation.notes: T1 (§3.1 amendment) shipped via fitme-story commit dd87bad. T2-T4 (Figma frame authoring) deferred to a dedicated Figma MCP session — portable design-build prompt shipped via fitme-story commit bf0b998. T5-T11 are blocked on T2-T4 node IDs landing first. Next session: pick execution path (A=/design build, B=dedicated Figma session, C=subagent dispatch, D=manual operator).

rationale_summary: The Internal-deferral policy at fitme-story/docs/CONTRIBUTING-design-system.md §3 says control-room components stay code-first because they are 'heavily data-driven — designing them in Figma without realistic data fixtures produces low-fidelity stubs.' The 2 UCC sign-in pages (/control-room/sign-in + /control-room/sign-in/recover) are form-driven not data-driven: stable email-input + WebAuthn-button UI + 8 known error states, no dynamic data fixtures needed. They also render to UNAUTHENTICATED users (effectively a public-feeling surface). UU1 (coral-pulse polish) already established that form-aesthetic matters here. This enhancement adds a 'form-driven exception' clause to §3 + ships Figma representation for the 2 sign-in screens + AuthPasskeyForm component + Code Connect mappings, closing the loop the parent feature deferred at ship 2026-05-07.

tasks.title: Amend fitme-story/docs/CONTRIBUTING-design-system.md §3 with 'form-driven exception' clause
tasks.result_note: §3.1 form-driven exception clause added to CONTRIBUTING-design-system.md (+ §1 decision tree update in lockstep). Initial qualifying list locks: AuthPasskeyForm + 2 sign-in pages. fitme-story commit dd87bad.
tasks.note: Adds an explicit exception clause: control-room components MAY have Figma representation when (a) shown to unauthenticated users OR (b) form-driven with no data-fixture complexity. Defines which components qualify (initial list: AuthPasskeyForm, plus the 2 sign-in pages). Defines what stays Internal (TaskTree, AuditLogPanel, DevicesTable, etc.). Cross-references the manifest convention update in T8.
tasks.related_prs_note: Cross-repo cites covering the full UCC narrative in the shared parent case study. Direct shippers of THIS feature: fitme-story #123 + #125 (2026-05-19). Sibling shippers cited in same case study: fitme-story #122 (redis-fix sibling), FT2 #163 (auth-polish-v2 ancestor), FT2 #248 (parent ucc-passkey-auth). Listed here to satisfy Q6 bidirectional PR-parity check across the shared case study.

tasks.note: Component variants matching the Status type from AuthPasskeyForm.tsx ('idle' | 'pending' | 'success' | 'error' | 'locked'). Light + dark mode pair. Variant property: mode = authenticate | register (controls primary button label). Component becomes the Figma source-of-truth referenced from the 2 page frames in T2/T3.
